name: Parque Natural de la Serra d'Irta — Vuelta desde Peñíscola por el Castillo de Xivert
region_intro: Mountain biking around Peñíscola / Peñíscola offers a diverse landscape characterized by coastal scenery and mountainous terrain. The region features the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, an unspoiled coastal mountain range with cliffs, coves, and Mediterranean vegetation. Trails often present rocky and dry conditions, with options ranging from relaxed coastal rides to technical mountain sections. This environment provides varied routes for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Peníscola / Peñíscola The most popular mountain bike trail is Parque Natural de la Serra d'Irta — Vuelta desde Peñíscola por el Castillo de Xivert, a 31.0 miles (50.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, offering panoramic views and passing Xivert Castle. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Torre Badum and Cala Argilaga – Coastal path loop from Marjal de Peníscola, a difficult 30.4 miles (48.9 km) path. This route follows a coastal path, featuring views of Torre Badum and the coves of Cala Argilaga. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Torre Badum – Coastal path loop from Marjal de Peníscola, a 22.8 miles (36.8 km) trail leading through coastal paths, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Mountain biking around Peñíscola / Peñíscola is defined by coastal paths, mountainous terrain, and the natural beauty of the Sierra de Irta Natural Park. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Peñíscola / Peñíscola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 350 reviews. More than 1700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Peñíscola / Peñíscola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Peníscola / Peñíscola, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: Peníscola / Peñíscola offers over 50 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 12 easy, 32 moderate, and 9 difficult options available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: The terrain in Peníscola / Peñíscola is diverse, characterized by a mix of coastal scenery and mountainous sections within the Sierra de Irta Natural Park. You can expect loose, rocky, and dry conditions, with some technical sections, as well as flowy singletracks. Many trails are multi-use, shared with hikers and trail runners.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes suitable for beginners in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: Yes, there are 12 easy mountain bike routes in the area. These trails typically offer less elevation gain and smoother paths, making them ideal for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, consider routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930644354/parque-natural-de-la-serra-d-irta-vuelta-desde-peniscola-por-el-castillo-de-xivert" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serra d'Irta — Vuelta desde Peñíscola por el Castillo de Xivert&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 50 km trail traversing the Sierra de Irta Natural Park with significant elevation. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31446163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre Badum and Cala Argilaga – Coastal path loop from Marjal de Peníscola&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 48.9 km coastal path with notable climbs.

Q: What natural landmarks or historical sites can I see while mountain biking in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: Many routes offer views of significant landmarks. You can cycle past the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3014731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñíscola Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a fortress built by the Knights Templar. Trails within the Sierra de Irta Natural Park often provide glimpses of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/244763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Xivert&lt;/a&gt; and coastal features like Torre Badum. The park itself is a natural treasure with virgin coves and Mediterranean vegetation.

Q: Are there any routes that offer particularly good coastal views?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra de Irta Natural Park boasts 13 kilometers of untouched coastline, and many mountain bike routes run alongside it. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40370927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí del Pebret Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 27.7 km ride known for its spectacular sea views. Another excellent option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40384274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Torre Badum – Coastal path loop from Marjal de Peníscola&lt;/a&gt;, which follows coastal paths with great vistas.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: Peníscola / Peñíscola enjoys an excellent Mediterranean climate with approximately 270 clear days per year. Spring and autumn are ideal for mountain biking, as the temperatures are milder and more comfortable for longer rides, helping to avoid the summer heat.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in the area?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed, the region offers 12 easy-rated trails that would be suitable for families with some biking experience. These routes generally have less challenging terrain and elevation, providing a more relaxed experience for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on mountain bike trails in the Sierra de Irta Natural Park?
A: The Sierra de Irta Natural Park generally allows dogs, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check specific regulations for the park before heading out, as rules can vary.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 350 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscape, the stunning coastal views, and the variety of trails that cater to different skill levels, especially within the Sierra de Irta Natural Park.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that form a loop?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Peníscola / Peñíscola are designed as loops. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31446163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre Badum and Cala Argilaga – Coastal path loop from Marjal de Peníscola&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40385393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Torre Badum – La Casita Rest Area loop from Restaurante Font Nova&lt;/a&gt; are popular loop options, allowing you to start and finish at the same point.

Q: Is parking available at trailheads for mountain biking in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near popular trailheads, especially those leading into the Sierra de Irta Natural Park or starting points within Peníscola itself. Specific parking locations can often be found on detailed route maps or local tourism information.

https://www.comunitatvalenciana.com/es/castello-castellon/peniscola-peniscola/fiestas/romeria-a-sant-antoni

One of the best restored and preserved castles in the Valencian Community. It is an Islamic fortification dating from the 10th-11th centuries. Located at the top of the Xivert mountain, today within the perimeter of the Parc Natural de la Serra d'Irta, the castle offers impressive views. There are also indications of the former existence of a Bronze Age settlement prior to the Muslim occupation that used it for defense and surveillance. Later the Templars added various architectural elements such as two large circular towers, a church and a cistern. Over time, the castle went through several Orders and lost importance, which led to its deterioration until it was decided to preserve it and later restore it during the present century.
